_README: The regression gate. A >20% p99 regression vs this is a HARD BUILD FAILURE (constitution Principle 1). Modified ONLY by the deliberate /perf-baseline command (DR-7) -- never automatically, and never as a side effect of a change that made things slower. A gate that absorbs whatever the code does is not a gate.
hardware_summary: Apple M4, 10 cores (4P+6E), 16GB, macOS 26.5.2 arm64, Apple clang 21.0.0, -O3 -DNDEBUG
caveats: NOT END-TO-END. This measures the matching call in isolation -- no journal, no gateway, no ring buffer. It is not comparable to an end-to-end figure and must never be presented as one. The end-to-end number arrives with Spec 009.

NOT DURABLE. No journal is in this path. When the >=1M orders/sec throughput headline is produced in Spec 009 it will also be a no-durability number, because fsync costs tens of microseconds and a million per second is physically impossible on any storage device. That caveat is permanent.

NO CORE ISOLATION. macOS provides no taskset/numactl/SCHED_FIFO equivalent, so the matching thread shares a core with the OS. The tail (p99/p999/max) therefore includes scheduling noise and is a pessimistic bound, not the engine's true tail.

CLOCK GRANULARITY IS ~41ns, which is COARSER THAN A SINGLE submit() CALL (~11ns). Per-call timing is therefore unresolvable on this platform and would report a meaningless quantized distribution. These figures are measured over batches of 64 orders and divided. Cross-validated against Google Benchmark (11.7 ns/op) -- the two instruments agree, which is why these numbers are trusted.

COORDINATED OMISSION does not apply to this straight-line loop (CO is a property of rate-driven measurement). It becomes a real hazard with the load generator in Spec 009, where hdr_record_corrected_value() becomes mandatory. Do not read this baseline as evidence that CO has been handled -- it is merely absent.
